contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert liters to gallons?
contentBlocks.html:
Liter (L) is the standard volume unit in the metric system. US gallon = 3.78541 liters. UK (imperial) gallon = 4.54609 liters. This converter uses US gallons by default.

This conversion is essential for fuel consumption (L/100km vs mpg), cooking, aquariums, and understanding liquid volumes in US contexts.

Fuel examples: a 50 L tank = 13.2 US gallons. Fuel consumption: 7 L/100km ≈ 33.6 mpg (US).

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 1. Enter a value
items.description: Type a number in the input field. You can use a period or comma as the decimal separator.

items.title: 2. Read the result
items.description: The conversion result appears instantly in the field next to it – no clicking required.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

contentBlocks.title: How does liters to gallons conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
1 US gallon = 231 cubic inches = 3.785411784 liters (exact). 1 UK gallon = 4.54609 liters.

To convert liters to US gallons, divide by 3.78541. To convert US gallons to liters, multiply by 3.78541.

Quick estimate: divide liters by 4 for a rough US gallon count (slightly overestimates).

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• US gallon = 3.785 L. UK gallon = 4.546 L (20% larger!).
  • 1 US gallon = 4 quarts = 8 pints = 128 fl oz.
  • Milk: US gallon jug = 3.785 L. UK: sold in pints (568 mL).
  • Fuel: 1 L/100km ≈ 235.2/x mpg (US). 7 L/100km = 33.6 mpg.

items.question: How many liters is 1 US gallon?
How many liters is 1 UK gallon?
items.answer: 1 UK (imperial) gallon = 4.54609 liters. UK gallons are about 20% larger than US gallons.

items.question: How do I convert L/100km to mpg?
items.answer: Divide 235.215 by L/100km. Example: 8 L/100km = 235.215/8 = 29.4 mpg (US).

items.question: How big is a 55-gallon drum in liters?
Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
